trials.supervisor_analysis: Stage 1 baseline/profile is complete. Baseline established at 0.816ms PyTorch, current best 0.745ms Triton (score 60.90). Profiling shows Conv3d at 59.4%, elementwise at 12.3%, transpose at 11.4%, InstanceNorm at 8.4%. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: You've spent significant time debugging the Triton instance norm stats kernel. Consider a pragmatic shortcut: create a working `ModelNew` that uses PyTorch for conv/multiply/instance_norm and only fuses the simpler clamp+multiply+max into a Triton kernel. This gets you a passing score and baseline quickly. Once you have a working solution, you can profile to see if instance norm is even the bottleneck before investing more time optimizing it.

Your fused Triton kernel's grid of `(batch_size, spatial_size)` = 1.6M programs has excessive launch overhead. Instead, use a 1D grid of `(batch_size * cdiv(spatial_size, BLOCK_SPATIAL),)` where each program handles `BLOCK_SPATIAL` spatial positions (e.g. 256), and for each position loads all 16 channels, applies clamp+multiply, and computes max — this reduces programs to ~6400. Also, as a quick alternative, try `torch.compile(mode="max-autotune")` on the forward method first to establish what's achievable before writing custom kernels.
